desc:
The trail bends south and west here, around a large pillar of black rock
standing at the side of the road. To the south the trail seems to make a sharp
bend, and in the west the trail continues, rising up the side of a far off
mountain. The pillar of rock here appears to have been crudely carved in the
shape of a humanoid, wearing some sort of crown, but the ravages of the weather
make further detail impossible to see.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
You can travel along this rocky path to the east and west, or you can rejoin the
main road in the north. Underfoot you hear the crunch of fine rocks and gravel,
and this path certainly looks like it has been built by someone or something.
Further evidence of habitation is given by the smoke curling lazily from the
chimney of the small hut to the south.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
Rocks and stones crunch happily under your feet as you travel along this path.
In the west the Misty Mountains reach up to touch the clouds, while closer you
can see a small wooden hut situated just south of here. You can go east and west
along this trail.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
The trail leads east and west. In the west there stands an imposing black pillar
of rock, that seems to have some sort of discernible shape, but it is too far
away for its exact shape to be seen. To the east the path carries on further
into the foothills, in the west the path bends to the north.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
The trail leads east and west here, and in the west there stands a large pillar
of black stone, silhouetted against the white background of the snow-capped
misty mountains. North of you rises an impenetrable wall of rock, while in the
south the mountains fall away into a valley before rising again to form a
magnificent line of peaks.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
To the north you can see a narrow valley between two high mountain peaks. The
trail continues to the west, to the High Pass, and to the east, down the
mountain slope. You notice a trampled path leading north into the small valley.
Examining it closer, you understand that it is well-used.
name: Small Crossing

desc:
The road takes you over a ridge. Occasional tufts of grass are spread along the
road. There is no other vegetation in this very hostile environment.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
The path ends in the south at the entrance to that neat little hut which you
have been admiring for so long. You can also follow the path to the west, where
it rejoins the main road. Looking west you again stare in wonder at the great
magnificence of those towering mountains.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
What used to be a road leading up the steep slope is almost completely gone. The
remainders of it are sprinkled with fallen rocks, making movement very
cumbersome.
name: Sharp Bend

desc:
The terrain to the east is far too rough to travel, and you can only proceed by
going up the mountain side, or further into the mountains to the west. To the
north, a high mountain rises, and looking south you see another of the high
peaks of the Hithaeglir.
name: Sharp Bend

desc:
Entering through the mouth of the valley to the east, the road climbs steadily
up the southern mountain slope, faithfully following all the bends of the
mountainside.
name: Road through the Lower Pass

desc:
The road is quite intact here, making travel a lot easier. It runs along the
southern side of the mountain, allowing you to climb the pass to the west or to
leave the mountains and head to the Anduin vale somewhere east of here. The
mountainside to the north looks very smooth.
name: Road through the Lower Pass

desc:
You are on a stony path winding downwards with a rocky wall to the north. To the
south the ground slopes away and there is a dell below the level of the path
overhung with bushes and low trees.
name: Road through the Lower Pass

desc:
You have now reached the bottom of the valley. To the north, a rather high cliff
towers above you. From the south, you hear the splashing of water, and looking
there, you see the source of a small stream, flowing slowly along the road to
the east. To the west a narrow trail leads up to an even narrower gap in the
Misty Mountains.
name: Road through the Lower Pass

desc:
The trail bends sharply here, rising higher to the west. East and south there is
a small cliff, below which can be seen a rapidly moving mountain stream.
Northward, the path continues its journey among the eastern foothills of the
Misty Mountains.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The road has now left the high mountains to the west and allows you to travel
much more easily. To the east you see a road intersection in the middle of the
wilderness. On the north side of the road pleasant green slopes invite you to
come and visit.
name: Road to the Lower Pass

desc:
This small enclosed yard lies adjacent to a crude homestead, and obviously
provides shelter to a few of the animals kept by whomever dwells in the cabin.
The only fencing is along the northern edge of the pen - in every other
direction natural granite forms the sides.
name: Animal Pen

desc:
The path ends here in a small, tidy garden, with most of the plants being in a
rockery, surprisingly enough. The hut to the west looks solidly built from wood,
which must have been hauled here from the forest you spotted way in the north,
and smoke curls lazily away from the chimney on a light breeze.
name: End of the Path

desc:
To the west of you is the Wolf Gate, the main entrance to the fabled
Goblin-gate, home of the orcs of the Misty Mountains. A few steps are running
down from the gate into a narrow valley between tall mountains.
name: At the Wolf Gate

desc:
The stupid orc who used to live here got lost in the mountains years ago. His
friends are still looking for him. If you don't like orcs, better not to be here
when they show up. The walls and the floor of the room are bare; no objects can
be seen, yet this place is obviously not totally forgotten. There are many
cracks in the walls and the floor. In a big crack on the ground there is some
water. Another big crack leads south.
name: Abandoned Hovel

desc:
It is an extremely steep and slippery path leading southwards. An other option
is to go through the big crack to the west. There is no sign of any life except
for lichens on the dark rocks.
name: Steep Path

desc:
This small valley is nestled in the foothills of the Misty Mountains, and it has
a quiet unspoiled demeanour. On either side steep walls guard its perimeter -
the only means of egress appears to be a small tunnel behind a rock to the
south.
name: Hidden Valley

desc:
You have to move very carefully so you don't disturb the rocks on the unstable
slope. The cliffs to the north and the south look impassable, but further to the
east a gap between them allows a view of a forested landscape.
name: Trail to the High Pass

desc:
A pile of rocks blocks your way to the east. Choked by the fallen rocks and
boulders, the trail forces its way down the steep slope.
name: End of the Broken Trail

desc:
Large rocks cover the western and the northern sides of this strange hill. Some
hands must have put the rocks in their places. Hands belonging to living or
undead creatures... who can tell?
name: Small Hill

desc:
The air is thick, making it hard to breathe. A weakhearted person or creature
might have to fight feelings of terror. What happened to the poor soul buried
here? This is not a burial place of anyone who was liked by his people. The head
has been cut of from the body and turned upside down, and through the heart an
iron spike has been driven.
name: Old Tomb

desc:
The pleasant grassy slope leads down from the small hill to the road. Looking
east, you can see before you only ridges and slopes falling towards lowlands and
plains glimpsed occasionally between the trees.
name: Green Slope

desc:
The road can take you north, to the distant Eagle's Eyrie and the small trading
centre Maethelburg that lies by the river Anduin. To the east you see the border
of a dark forest, and south of here is a small crossing.
name: Maethel Road

desc:
The road from Maethelburg connects here to the road leading to the Lower Pass
and to the Old Dwarven Road which starts to the east of here.
name: Crossing

desc:
You are on the remnants of the Old Dwarven Road which connects Mount Erebor far
in the east with Eriador to the west. Now only the fearless Beornings prevent
the road from disappearing completely. A bit west the road splits into two
parts, each leading through one branch of the High Pass.
name: North of a Ford

desc:
You are on the western border of a dark forest. The trees are standing very
closely here, and the thick undergrowth makes it hard to move. West and south
you can catch a glimpse of the road leading through the forested landscape. To
the east the forest gets thicker.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Pines, firs and other trees stand together in this quiet forest. You hear no
birds and no animals. You watch carefully for any sign of life, knowing that
something is here, waiting for its prey in the shadows.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
The forest is here, quietly abiding its time. You keep turning around to check
if anything has sneaked up on you from behind. From the treetops you hear the
rattling of the leaves in the light breeze from the valley of the great river
Anduin somewhere in the east.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
This is the east rim of the dark pine forest at the foot of the mountain range.
Trees obscure your view to the west, and it seems to you that there is something
moving in their shadow. To the south you can see a small forest river.
name: Forest

desc:
This mixed forest at the foot of the Misty Mountains consists of tall pines, old
oaks and stout elms. Just south of here, you hear the sound of running water. It
is a small stream carrying fresh water from the mountains to feed the Anduin
somewhere in the south.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Here the Old Dwarven Road leads across the little forest stream running east
from the mountains towards the Anduin. The water looks cold, clear and quite
safe to drink.
name: The Ford

desc:
To the north, the road winds its way into the lower branch of the High Pass, and
to the south, it can take you down to the great river Anduin. To the east is a
dark, green forest.
name: South of a Ford

desc:
This very old road was once built by dwarves to support trade between Arnor and
the Lonely Mountain. Now it is mainly used by foul creatures of the night. The
road leads north and south passing a slope you can see if you look west. On the
east side is a green but very dark forest.
name: Old Dwarven Road

desc:
The old road turns north and east here. To the east of the road the forest is so
thick that you can barely see two steps ahead. To the west the pine-forest rises
almost to the top of the mountain slope.
name: Bend in the Old Dwarven Road

desc:
This is yet another turn in the twisting road, which is getting more and more
overgrown. Obviously, travellers are not common guests here. Maybe the
inhabitants of the surrounding forests do not need tracks or roads?
name: Old Dwarven Road

desc:
The road bends north and east here. Green hills rise to the west of the road,
their slopes covered by various trees and bushes. Some tall birches grow just to
the south of the road. To the northeast you can see a dark forest.
name: Old Dwarven Road

desc:
Zigzagging its way through a dark forest, this overgrown road can take you into
the Misty Mountains, or south towards the Anduin. On all sides, high trees
surrounds you.
name: Old Dwarven Road

desc:
The road makes its way through a very dark forest. It is possible to continue on
the road north and east. A thick forest to the south does not let anyone pass.
The trees to the west are less dense.
name: Old Dwarven Road

desc:
The road makes its way through a dark forest towards an unknown destiny in the
east. Looking along the road, you can see that it leads down to the river
Anduin, which it crosses by a ford, known to be rather dangerous. Beyond the
river the road leads into Mirkwood and to the lands beyond.
name: Road to the Old Ford

desc:
The trees that stand here are worn and scarred, like weary soldiers after a
bloody battle. Many other trees lie on the ground; some have been cut, some
broken, and some uprooted altogether. Smaller branches and twigs litter the
area, with dead leaves and wood splinters covering the earth. As the wind blows
by, it creates a low, resonating sound, with the pitch shifting up as the wind
dies down. This place looks like a battlefield, as if the forces of nature had
met in some sort of violent clash, only to have a tragic finale ending in sorrow
and despair.
name: Bleeding Forest

desc:
The forest here thins, allowing much more light to enter compared to the darker
surrounding forest. Most of the trees seem to have been growing here a long
while, although some are younger, about twice as tall as an adult human. The
lower density of the forest gives room for some shrubs to grow. Some of the
bushes have little round berries, although they do not look like they would be
very tasty. The foliage all around has a medium green hue, but there is a great
variety in the shapes of the leaves.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The stream takes a sharp bend here, flowing beneath a small cliff to the north.
The water is frigid, fed by snowmelt from the peaks to the far west. To the
east, the stream widens a bit and becomes shallower before disappearing into a
forest.
name: Mountain Stream

desc:
This frigid mountain stream swifly plunges through a series of waterfalls and
rapids on its journey to the Anduin. There is a hairpin turn here, and the banks
are so steep that it is very hard to find purchase there. To the north the
terrain becomes more gentle, but the current is still very fast.
name: Cascades

desc:
The stream plunges through a series of short waterfalls. The current is very
swift, and the channel is full of sharp rocks. Northward there is a small cliff,
above which runs a road. To the west, the land tilts sharply upwards, rising to
the first peaks of the Mountains. You can climb the rocky bank to the south.
name: Cascades

desc:
The one who cups his hand for a sip and plunges it in the water might quickly
pull it back. The water is freezing even during the summer. On the north and the
south banks huge trees guard the river. Looking upstream, to the west, you see a
small path crossing the stream.
name: Forest River

desc:
The small river cheerfully carries its waters through the sombre forest. The low
mossy banks are covered with tall pine-trees which drop their cones, needles and
branches into the clear water of the current.
name: Forest River

desc:
Here the small river leaves the surrounding forest and makes its way through the
grassy plains west of the river Anduin. The water is clear and seems quite safe
to drink.
name: Forest River

desc:
The river that flows from the west turns south here, trying to escape the
clutches of the dark forest in the north. It rushes toward the grasslands to the
south-east in its journey towards the great river Anduin. Sloping gently, the
water carries some debris, but becomes ever more clear as it progresses
downstream. Some gnarled logs create a maze for the water to run through, as if
the logs were the arms of the forest, trying in vain to hinder the river's flow.
name: Forest River

desc:
The river flows in from the north, abruptly changing course to head eastwards,
as if it couldn't make up its mind about what to do. The southern edge of the
river bank at the bend is rocky and the water swells and gurgles as it lashes
against the rocks. A dark forest looms ominously to the south threatening to
devour the river.
name: Bend in the Forest River

desc:
The stream continues its sinuous course in search of the mighty flow of the
Anduin. In this area the river opens its way into a dark, sinister-looking
forest. Lofty trees with greyish trunks and blackened leaves loom by the
riverside. Leaning over the waters, they cast thick shadows on the darkened
surface; even the gurgling of the stream can barely be heard here, as if the
sickly-looking barks absorbed the lively sound of the running water.
name: Forest River

desc:
The river makes a bend here, turning east. Upstream to the north the river is
cloaked in the darkness of the forest. On this new eastward course light can be
seen; a refreshing change from the dreary forest. As the river turns against the
bank it quickly picks up speed, the sound of the churning water drowns out any
noise of wildlife; thunder is perhaps the only thing that might be heard over
the water.
name: Swift Forest River

desc:
Free of the dark forest, the river is wider and more approachable. From the
grassy banks the slow trickle of water can be heard. At this point, the river
turns southward. The rocks in the riverbed are worn smooth and round from
decades of the water running slowly over them. The surrounding plains have been
soaked by the rivers' runoff.
name: Emerging River

desc:
As the river bends towards the south it widens slightly. Although slow flowing
up until this point the slope of the ground to the south-east pulls the water
downstream more forcefully. The current is increasingly stronger as it rushes to
meet the much larger Anduin River in the distance. The shadow of the forest to
the west is now just a memory, as this river heads toward brighter plains and
pastures.
name: Small River

desc:
As the river bends eastward the current begins to pick up speed. While the
northern portion of this segment of river is relatively calm, the eastern
section rushes at quite a quick pace. The fervent sounds of rushing water can be
heard coming from that direction. Westward a dark forest looms, casting an
ominous shadow upon the land.
name: Turn in the River

desc:
The river slows a bit here as it turns to the south, but it is not by any means
tranquil. Looking upstream to the west there is a dark forest in the distance.
Toward the east there are some ruins of what could have been a house, now
reduced to rubble. Whatever destroyed that place is unknown. However, it was
certainly not natural erosion.
name: Fast Flowing River

desc:
At this bend the river makes a quick turn backward to the east, part of a series
of turns that serve to speed up the current even more. The loud roar of the
rushing water is overwhelming and the banks of the river are under tremendous
pressure as they force the strong river to turn at a sharp angle. Anyone
travelling by boat here would have to keep their focus as they navigate these
waters, lest they be dashed against the banks or capsized by the force of the
water. A swimmer could do nothing but let the strongest current carry him where
it may.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
This small forest at the foot of the mountains is full of green trees and
colourful wild flowers. Listening to the singing birds and admiring the beauty
of the forest makes you want to sit down and take a break from the dangerous
world around you.
name: Dark Green Forest

desc:
You are now deep within the forest. Tall pines and sturdy oaks surround you, and
you have trouble moving around due to the thick undergrowth. Listening
carefully, you hear a grunting sound. Was it a boar, or something bigger
sneaking around looking for fresh meat?
name: Deep inside the Forest

desc:
You are now deep inside the forest. Old rotten logs, mixed with dead leaves and
some brightly coloured flowers lie among the high trees. It is very dark in
here. You unsuccessfully try to find the sources of the sounds that reach you.
You can hear boars grunting, birds singing and an occasional branch breaking.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest surrounding you is very old. Ancient trees loom above you, forming an
impenetrable ceiling between you and the sky. Moss-covered stones and the
remnants of long dead trees make it very hard to move.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The trees in this area of the forest seem younger than in other parts of it, but
still have weathered many seasons. Their lush foliage blocks out any natural
sunlight. The floor of the forest consists mostly of fallen leaves and twigs.
Smaller plants and shrubs would be choked out by the massive roots of the
towering trees, and would not receive the light they need to survive. The same
might be true for other things that enter here.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest is thick as the trees crowd each other. The trees are an even mixture
of old, wizened ones and younger, less hardened ones. With the passing wind the
tree branches sway, looking as if they are talking to each other; it is almost
possible to hear them whispering.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Despite the great darkness covering much of this entire area, here some light
from the sky manages to break through. The illumination is enough to see clearly
during the day, or with a full moon at night. The stands of trees, while
predominantly young, include some larger, older trees as well. The unusual quiet
of this place, coupled with the uneven shafts of light entering from above,
combine to create a very eerie feeling suggesting this would not make a good
resting place.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
In this part of the forest there is a great variety among the trees. Some seem
to be quite old, having weathered many winters, and grown tall with the light of
the spring. Others are shorter, but still seem as if they have been here for
ages. If only one could know what these trees have seen, for surely they must
have been growing here since the beginning of time.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Several large trees stand around this glade, providing a natural cloak. The
ground is worn and packed, as if many large and heavy creatures had travelled
through here. Some trees bear large scratches like scars, evidence that some of
the visitors were quite fierce creatures with large, sharp claws. If those
creatures were marking their territory it might be unwise to stay here for long
lest they return.
name: Wolf Glade

desc:
The forest is more dense. High trees mixed with lower brush form a very hard
terrain to move in. Looking around you, you see the tracks of wild animals such
boars and deers. You also see tracks of something bigger - man or beast...
Looking back, you get the comforting view of the road to the south and the west
and realise that at least you are not hopelessly lost yet.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
This dark and gloomy forest covers some of the foothills of the Misty mountains.
Somewhere to the east, the land gradually flattens into the Vale of Anduin, but
here it is still quite rugged.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
The thick undergrowth is making it hard to walk. To the north the forest is so
dense that you can barely see two steps ahead. The trees grow less dense south
and west.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Trees surround you on all the sides, hiding the sky above. The forest is dark
and gloomy with thick undergrowth, making it very hard to move around. All
around you, you hear the sounds of the forest, birds singing and boars grunting.
name: Deep inside the Forest

desc:
To the north and to the east you can see nothing but tall green trees. West and
south you can catch a glimpse of an overgrown road making its way through the
forest.
name: Dark Green Forest

desc:
Under an overhanging cliff, there is a dirty, smelly, stinking orc camp among
sharp rocks. Filthy piles of debris lies around it, and some unsanitary pots
with black, oily stews can be seen hanging over fires.
name: Orc Camp

desc:
Wow! The view from here is breathtaking. You have reached one of the lower
eastern ridges of the Misty Mountains. Looking east, you see a green forest and
beyond it the mighty Anduin with its surrounding lowlands. Even further away
lies a dark-green sea, the Mirkwood. To the west you see a deep valley far
below.
name: Mountain Ridge

desc:
The ridge turns west-south here. To the west you can see its crest and the
snow-capped peaks of the mountains beyond. To the east and the north you can try
to make your way down the rocky slopes to the green valleys below.
name: Mountain Ridge

desc:
You are climbing up the steep rocky slope to the top of the mountain ridge. The
rough path, which has been leading up the slope, has disappeared. The bushes and
the long grasses between the boulders, the patches of rabbit-cropped turf and
the bright flowers have all vanished. Pebbles, rubbish, rocks and boulders cover
the slope in uneven piles, as if waiting for something or someone to start an
avalanche.
name: Mountain Slope

desc:
You are on the slope which leads to the top of a mountain ridge. A few bushes
are the only larger growing things here. A small forest can be seen below the
ridge to the east.
name: Mountain Slope

desc:
You are on the top of a wide steep slope of fallen stones, the remains of a
landslide. When you try to move down it, rubbish and small pebbles roll away
from your feet. Soon larger bits of split stone go clattering down and start
other pieces below them slithering and rolling, and you are forced to walk very
carefully to avoid setting the whole slope above and below you on the move.
name: Top of the Mountain Slope

desc:
You have finally reached the eastern and lower side of the ridge. Looking around
you, you see the Misty Mountains to the west, and a small forest below you to
the northeast. A few dwarf birches are the only growing things up here except
for the low grass that covers the ridge.
name: Mountain Ridge

desc:
You have stumbled across a small path among the rocks. It leads east, from where
you can hear thick voices speaking a foul orkish dialect.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
Slender white birches rise all around you, a marvel in this dully coloured
landscape. To the north and east you can see the old dwarven road running along
the border of a dense forest. The ground is very wet. To the west there is an
opening between the trees that does not look natural.
name: Birches

desc:
You have reached the crest of the ridge. The steep mountain slopes facing west,
south and north make this place look like a fortress. The air is thin and clear,
and even the smell of the pine trees does not reach so far up in the mountains.
During the short and intense spring, the mountain flowers celebrate it with
colours and smells.
name: Crest of the Ridge

desc:
This forest, on the eastern foothills of the Misty Mountains consists mainly of
high, strong pines, mixed with an occasional birch and some oaks. Every step you
take brings you further up on the mountains, that rise to heights of over
eighteen thousand feet above you.
name: Small Forest

desc:
You are on the edge of a climbing wood of pines that stand here right up the
mountain slope from the deeper darker forests of the valleys below. Looking
west, you can see the rocky slope continue till it reaches the top of the
mountain ridge. A path goes down the slope to the south.
name: Edge of a Small Forest

desc:
You are on the edge of a green pine forest at the eastern foothills of the
largest mountain-range in all Arda - the Misty Mountains. Just east of here you
can barely make out a small road. To the west the terrain starts to slope up.
Some tracks lead south.
name: Pine Forest

desc:
The slope is covered with fir trees that provide some shelter from the wind. To
the north you can see the top of a cliff and a series of waterfalls below. To
the east, the trees give way to a road, and beyond the road there is a dense
forest. To the west the ground becomes more rocky.
name: Slope above a Stream

desc:
Rocks of all possible shapes and sizes are strewn around you. A couple of fir
trees decorate the monotonous landscape. To the east, the ground slopes up a
little. To the north there is a steep drop to a small stream. To the south the
slope rises all the way to the crest of the mountain ridge.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The path turns west-south here, rising up the rocky slope to the south. Pebbles
and lumps of rock litter the path, making it hard to move. Some boulders rise on
the sides of the path, giving a shelter from the wind and falling rocks to a few
patches of grass and an occasional bright flower. To the north you can see a
small stream.
name: Rough Path

desc:
The path leaves the small dell to the north and winds its way up the green
slope. Bushes and boulders seem to be all around you, both beside the path and
almost directly under your feet, as if trying to make you stumble. You notice
some plants and berries which look as if they might be edible.
name: Rough Path

desc:
Bushes and low trees overhang the dell which lies just below the level of the
road through the Lower Pass. Long bright-green grasses cover the ground like a
thick carpet with a couple of thinner patches where the grass has been cropped
by rabbits. Two big boulders rise at the northern end of the dell as if keeping
watch, the ground around them decorated with yellow rockroses. To the east you
can see the source of the small stream, the waters of which feed the grasses
around you. A small path leaves the dell to the south.
name: Small Dell

desc:
A small stream bubbles out of a little hole in the rocks to the west. It runs
along the road to the north. Green grass and occasional flowers surround it,
feeding on the nutritious fresh water.
name: Source of the Stream

desc:
The trail bends among the fallen rocks. To the south there is a sheer vertical
drop into a dim valley far below. The walls of the chasm are too steep to try to
climb down.
name: Narrow Part of the Broken Trail

desc:
You feel your knees wobbling as you try not to look down the steep cliff to the
south. Keeping yourself close to the mountain wall, you press on your hazardous
journey through the Misty Mountains. Looking behind a boulder that has fallen
down on the trail, you see a dark cave.
name: Trail at a Cave

desc:
After the long journey that brought you here, this dry cave really looks like
the ideal place to rest. A narrow arch, barely wide enough for an unloaded mount
to pass, is the only connection to the outer world, making the cave an efficient
shelter against bad weather and predator beasts.
name: Dark Dry Cave

desc:
To the south, the rock is marred by a large crack, into which has been wedged a
boulder that acts as a crude door. There is a small chink left free at the top,
so it is possible to look into the chamber beyond. The air flowing through the
crack is fresh, so the surface must be quite near. A narrow tunnel marked with
the tracks of iron shod boots leads down into some type of hewn chamber.
name: Behind the Crack

desc:
The Misty Mountains loom large and real in the south, overpowering your senses
with their vast presence. The path on which you are travelling is rocky and hard
to walk on, and the sun bleached bones of many animals lie by the wayside. The
path leads east and west here.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
Snow capped mountains dominate the landscape here, and they distract your eyes
from looking at what you are walking on, which is almost certainly not just
broken rocks and gravel. The trail bends east and north here.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
This difficult trail winds through a rock-strewn col - not a valley, but merely
a place lower than the mountains around it. Steep overhanging cliffs line the
path to the northwest and southeast. But for a few low trailing creepers eking
out a miserable existence among the rocks, the land seems completely barren.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
The Misty Mountains are lying before you like an enormous chain of mountains
continuing in a circle. The path on which you are travelling is rocky and hard
to walk on. The mountains around you force the trail to lead into a canyon here,
this place would be perfect for an ambush. It is difficult to see anything that
might be alive here, although, the bones of many animals are scattered by the
wayside. The path leads north and east.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
The trail continues into a small canyon to your south, leading to the Misty
Mountains. The trail is crooked and hard to walk on. Parts of dead animals are
scattered all around. The snow-white tips of the mountains around you catch your
eye. The trail turns east, or you can leave south.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
There are no more bushes or grass here. Only mountains and hilltops are left to
surround you like enormous shadows. The path itself is crooked and hard to walk
on, bones are covering the wayside.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
Rocks and hilltops surround you in every direction. This trail is very desolate.
Rocks and bones from dead animals surround you. Only a few scattered bushes can
be seen. The path goes north-south.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
You are walking on a small trail, leading around the mountain. This trail is
probably frequently used by travellers since it is well marked and easy to walk
upon. You can hear birds singing in the distance. This place seems to be a good
shelter from the wind. The trail continues in a west-north direction from here.
name: Trail around the Mountain

desc:
You are walking on a small trail, leading around the mountain. This trail is
probably frequently used by travellers since it is well marked and easy to walk
upon. To the west, you see huge rising mountains, while on your east side you
see small hills everywhere. The trail continues to the south, or you can enter
some hills to the north. A small path is leading to another trail east of you.
name: Trail around the Mountain

desc:
You notice the snow lying on the top of every mountain. A couple of birds are
flying high in the sky. Sometimes you can spot bones and skulls of different
animals lying beside the trail. You can either go north into some rocky hills,
or leave south. A small path leads to another trail west of you.
name: Broken Trail

desc:
You are standing on a rocky hilltop. There is nothing but solid rock around
here. Not even a tiny little green bush is to be seen, making this place look
very barren. Below you, a trail leads northwards. Further north, you see a lot
of mountains, and to the west you can see rising mountains. There seems to be a
trail to the west of you, and to the south is a very dark place.
name: On a Rocky Hill

desc:
This is a really dark place. The trees block all light from the sun, even when
the sun is at its brightest. This must be a perfect hiding place for creatures
that prefer shady or dark places during sunlight. A narrow gap among the trees
allows you to leave this place northwards.
name: Shady Place

desc:
You are walking on a well-prepared mountain trail. A high chain of mountains,
seemingly impassable, is west of you. You can see some grassy parts in the
lowlands around you, but mostly this place consists of rocks and pebbles in all
kind of different sizes and shapes. To the south the trail carefully slithers
its way towards the mountains, climbing up and down narrow valleys. Eastwards
you can see a hilltop.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
You are standing at the bottom of the mountainpeak. You will have to climb to
reach the top. There is a lot of snow covering the trail further up, so you will
have to force your way through if you really want to reach the upper point of
this area. You can go north on the trail, or you can leave up.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
You have now reached the top of this mountain, although you see that this is not
the highest peak around here. In the distant north you can see higher mountains,
each seeming taller than the previous one and many with very pointed peaks. This
tip, like most tips around, is covered with snow.
name: Top of a Mountain

desc:
You are now on the ridge of this mountain and there is snow everywhere. To the
north, you can see a large range of mountains rising up. On your west you see
far down into the lowlands. To your south, you see a steep slope that meanders
up to the highest peak of this mountain. You can climb down to the lowlands
here.
name: On a Mountain Ridge

desc:
This path is extremely steep. In fact, you spend more time climbing than
actually walking. This area is mostly covered with snow which makes every step
dangerous. To the west you can see a trail leading further down to the lowlands.
name: Steep Path to the Mountains

desc:
This trail takes a torturous path through the mountains. There are some low
trees and bushes around here, but the trail climbs tall mountains to the east
and the west of you, and you can spot snow on their higher slopes. Another
branch of the trail leads down into a valley to your north.
name: Winding Trail

desc:
You find yourself among the high mountains in the Misty Mountains. There are a
few trees around you, crooked, with a miserable look. The ground is covered with
a thin layer of soil, but beneath it you can feel the hard stone. East of you a
huge range of mountains are reaching towards the sky, with snowy peaks. The wind
here is chilly and it might be hard to find shelter if a storm would hit you.
name: A Trail

desc:
The mountain is sloping downwards to the west here, leading towards some
boulders that seems to hinder you from going that way. Somehow, nature seems to
force you to go south into a narrow pass, or walk up to the trail. Loose stones
and rocks are getting more and more frequent and it is getting hard to travel
fast. From time to time you can hear a bird squeeking loudly from above, but it
is hard to see the source of the noise.
name: A Small Crossing

desc:
This is an extremely narrow trail, which twists through the mountains. On all
sides there are huge mountains that reach as high up as the eye can see. Stones
can be heard falling down the sides, crushing other stones as they reach the
bottom. The passage is limited to travel either south or north, and hoping no
stones will fall during the journey. Every sound is amplified as it echoes
between the mountains.
name: A Narrow Trail

desc:
As you walk around a corner of this twisted, narrow mountaintrail, you find
yourself at a dead end. A huge sheer cliff stops you from walking any further.
As you stand still for a while, a deadly silence lingers in the air. At the base
of the cliffs around you, a few plants and flowers has found a refuge.
name: Dead End

desc:
Here, at the base of some huge cliffs reaching towards the sky, you find a small
stony shore at the edge of a mountain lake. There are lots of stones here,
making it hard for you to walk here. Some of them are pointed, but there are
also some round ones to be seen. The water south of you is dark and ominous,
however, the sharp cliffs on the other side which are mirrored in the surface,
gives you an extremely steady impression.
name: At the Base of a Cliff

desc:
This small dark lake lies between the high peaks of the Misty Mountains. Sharp
cliffs leads down into the dark, hostile water. North of you a stony shore is
hidden between the mountains. The air here is chilly and sometimes you hear
something reach the surface, to vanish as fast as the mirrorlike image of the
mountains in the water has been grumbled.
name: A Mountain Lake

desc:
Deep within the Misty Mountains, this oval mountain lake lies beneath rocky
cliffs. Its water is dark and ominous, making it impossible to see further than
a few yards in it. North of you, a stony shore is split into two by a sharp
cliff. Its sheer rockface makes a hard and undestroyable impression between the
two shores.
name: A Mountain Lake

desc:
This dark lake, hidden between the mountains, is surrounded by the sharp cliffs
of the mountains. A sharp pointed cliff is splitting the stony shore north of
you into two. The water is dark and ominous; it is impossible to see further
than a few feet in it.
name: A Mountain Lake

desc:
Here, beneath some thorny bushes, you find a stony shore. Pointed stones and
rocks makes it hard to walk here. Pieces of dead animals can be seen among the
stones. The shore is leading down to the dark waters south of you. It seems to
be a small lake lying here, deep within the Misty Mountains. The water looks
ominous, sometimes you can hear something reach the surface just to disappear as
fast as the image of the mountains on the other side has been grumbled. A small
dark entrance to a cave is lying north of you.
name: Beneath the Trail

desc:
Here, far below the trail you left, you find a small cave. You can see light
from outside south of you, and far away the black water of a lake. The cave
floor is cold and moistured, water is dripping from the sharp stones in the
wall. Shadows are flickering in the light, and sometimes the echoing sound of a
stone falling can be heard.
name: Under the Mountain

desc:
This pass is very narrow, but not impossible to walk through. To the north you
can spot a small crack in the massive wall of stone. This place is shaded by all
the mountains around you. East of you some hills are rising and a small path is
leading west.
name: Narrow Pass

desc:
In this dark and brooding valley, a deathly silence lingers. The smell in the
air is most foul, and leaves a dreadfully unpleasant taste in traveller's mouth.
name: Gloomy Valley

desc:
You are walking on some rolling hills. You can see many more hills to the south
of you. The landscape around you consists of hilltops, some of which are grassy,
others rocky. A strong wind is blowing from the mountaintops to the west of you.
You can spot snowclad peaks far away.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You are walking on some rolling hills. You can see many more hills to the south
of you. The landscape around you consists of hilltops, some which are grassy,
others rocky. You can spot snowclad peaks far away, and some birds flying high
in the sky.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You are walking on some rolling hills. You can see many more hills to the south
of you. The landscape around you consists of hilltops, some which are grassy,
others rocky. You can spot snowclad peaks far away, and some birds flying high
in the sky.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You are walking through some rolling hills. The landscape around you consists of
hilltops with some being grassy and some being rocky. West of you, huge
mountains rise and give the impression of a wall. A trail leads south, while to
the north you can see the appearance of more hills.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
The hills here are rough and rocky. The path you followed is impossible to
follow due to all the rocks and boulders of different sizes constantly
obstructing your way. You notice a tiny path seeming to go south. A gorge is
opening up to the north, making it impossible to go in that direction. There are
some less rocky hills to the west of you, while east of you the rocky hills seem
to continue.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The hills here have become much more rough and rocky. You have to take it real
slow up here, twisting your way between large boulders and stones. The
surrounding area looks pretty waste and dead, but to the east of you, you can
see more hills and some of them are also covered with green grass.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
You are standing in the middle of hills stretching both to the north and to the
south. The hills climb and fall endlessly, but you notice that you slightly move
upwards. There is no bird singing here, and it seems like the hills are watching
you in a strange way, like they seem to be disturbed by your presence. the
surrounding area looks rather waste, cold and dead.
name: Hills

desc:
All the small hills here surround you totally, making you a bit worried. The air
here seems to carry a strange feeling to you. Or maybe it is only the fresh wind
blowing steadily in your face all the time that annoys you. To the east you can
enter a steep slope, and in all other directions you can only see hills.
name: Hills

desc:
You are walking up and down on several small hilltops. The landscape around you
is changing. There are some spots of snow around here, and you also see a lot of
bushes and low trees around. To the west you can see a narrow pass, and to the
north you can see more hilltops.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
You are walking up and down on several small hilltops. The landscape around you
is changing. There are some spots of snow around here, and you also see a lot of
bushes and low trees around. There are a lots of birds high up in the sky. To
the far east you can see more lowlands. To the west you can see a big range of
mountains rising up.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
You are walking up and down on several small hilltops. The landscape around you
is changing. There is more vegetation here, and most rocks and pebbles here are
covered with moss. Southwards, you can see some big rocks, and to the west you
can spot more hilltops.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
You are in front of a mountain cabin. The cabin is nested among high rocks,
making it well-hidden in this mountain country. This cabin must have been used
by the mountaineers, maybe to give them shelter from the weather and a safe
place to sleep for the night. However it seems to have been a long time since
anyone visited this place.
name: In Front of a Mountain Cabin

desc:
The cabin seems to be abandoned a long time ago. The floor is rotten, and a foul
stench is here as well. There are no windows here, except a small one on the
northern side, making this place very dark. The room has no furniture and looks
very naked. In the far left corner you can see a hatch, probably leading down to
the cellar.
name: Abandoned Mountain Cabin

desc:
You are standing on a steep slope, leading down to some small hills. The ground
here is covered with grass, and you can even see a few bushes and low trees
around. To the west you can see more hills open up, and further west you can see
a huge range of mountains rising up. East of here you can see some occasional
mountains, but it seems like this must be the easternmost part of Misty
Mountains. North of you, you can see some rocks.
name: Steep Slope

desc:
You are walking among some grassy hilltops. There are no special path to walk
upon here, but you travel fast and easy here anyway. To the far west and south
you can see the dreadful and famous Misty Mountains rising up in the horizon.
The air seems to carry a strange feeling to you. You can enter a steep slope up
here, or you can enter more hills to the south.
name: Low Hills

desc:
You are standing between small hills covered with green grass. The hills climb
and fall endlessly, but you notice that you are moving slightly upwards when you
travel northwards. To the west and south of you, the hills look more rocky,
while northwards you can see more hills in front of you. There is a big gorge to
the south, making it impossible to travel that way.
name: Low Hills

desc:
The path here is extremely difficult to walk upon because of all the rocks and
boulders obstructing your way. This, however, seems to be a dead end. On the
north side, a large gorge is blocking your way. The eastern and southern areas
look too steep to travel down. The only exit is back west.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
This looks like the end of this tunnel. You can spot some different digging
equipment scattered on the ground. Some ragged clothes are lying in a heap in
the northwest corner. The smell of rotten flesh is very strong here. The only
exit is to the east.
name: End of Tunnel

desc:
You are standing in a large tunnel going east-west. It must be some kind of
escape route or hideout for the trolls during daylight. It is very dark here,
and the air is damp and chilly. A dribbling sound can be heard from the moist
walls of earth.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
This seems to be an underground tunnel system probably made by trolls, for use
by trolls. The presence of trolls is very strong and an odour of rotten flesh
pervades the air. A dribbling sound can be heard from the moistured earthwalls.
The tunnel continues to the west.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
You wonder if it was a good idea to do this climbing after all. When you look
upwards you only see more heights to be conquered by you, and you can also spot
the top far away. It seems like the snow is lying heavy on its shoulders. You
are getting cold here, as you are wading in the snow.
name: A Mountainside

desc:
At last you are standing on the highest peak in this area. The view here is
really incredible. To your north several peaks, almost as high as this one, form
a wall. It will not be easy to climb back down. All the snow here makes it
dangerous to move around and it probably doesn't take much to cause an
avalanche. The only exit is down.
name: Snowy Peak of the Mountain

desc:
The path leaves the shelter of the valley and leads downwards and out to the
road in the High Pass to the south. The path is obviously well-used and several
of tracks here come from hungry wargs.
name: Small Path

desc:
From the looks of it this field was once fertile and lush with wheat; now almost
everything has withered away. Stalks of wheat lay clumped in piles on the
ground, slowly decomposing into the earth. Not far to the north is a bend where
the river turns and makes its way eastward. A dark forest looms across the field
a ways to the west.
name: Withering Field

desc:
The trail meanders along, slowly leading eastwards. There is little sign of wear
on the ground leaving only a faint rut to indicate the path upon which grass is
taking hold. A river rushing nearby to the south fills the air with the furious
churning sound of fast-flowing water. The sound seems fainter to the north,
where the quiet plains stretch out into the distance.
name: Abandoned Trail

desc:
The land here is barren, with a mixture of large boulders and gravel scattered
about. The rocks are all that provides even a variety of colour. This area seems
quite inhospitable to any kind of life, be it plants or animals. To the east
there seem to be the ruins of a small village in the distance. To the south
there is an old disused trail.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The land is quite rocky, with huge stone shards jutting up out of the earth.
Travelling across this area is quite difficult as each possible path is
determined by the rocks which seem to create a confusing labyrinth. Someone on
foot might have an easier time hopping across the tops of the large boulders;
those with mounts, however, must travel on the ground.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Bundles of thick weeds grow here. The tangles easily trip up those walking
through. For young adventurers or those of short stature, travel here could be
markedly slow. Although the earth is fertile, there is no space for anything of
worth to grow; the weeds have overrun the field, choking out all other plants.
Digging into the ground a bit one only finds a tangle of thin roots and masses
of seedpods.
name: A Field of Weeds

desc:
The ground is quite uneven, riddled with large bumps and small rocks that could
slow down weary travellers. These rough plains are filled with uneven grasses
that grow in clumps of various sizes. A few wildflowers present small blotches
of colour breaking up the monotony of the landscape.
name: Bumpy Plains

desc:
Standing out among the low and widely scattered patches of bushes there is a
small cottage crouching beside a small and low hill. The back wall of the old
and abandoned farmstead is built into the small earth-mound, and the remains of
an old fence can be seen here and there.
name: Cottage in the Plains

desc:
Small bushes are scattered all over this section of the plains, like small
clouds spread throughout the sky on a midsummer day. Although this appears to be
a very fertile area few animals can be seen. To the east a small ruined cottage
stands out against the backdrop of this landscape. The grasslands slope upward
toward a large forest which can be seen far off to the west.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The plain slopes down to the south-east in a hardly perceptible manner. A number
of large bushes crown the tops of long but low-lying bumps that sit like islands
in the plain. The height of the bushes on top of the bumps almost makes the
plain a living maze for travellers trying to navigate through it.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The rhythmic undulating motion of these plains is reminiscent of a bunch of
rabbits frolicking in the grass; every so often there is a stretch where the
plains lie flat, as if taking a rest, before bounding off again into the
distance. Standing on one of the higher points allows a good view of the flowing
ripples of the surrounding land. As the wind blows the tips of the grass sway
like waves in the ocean.
name: Bouncing Plains

desc:
This lush field sports an abundance of long sturdy grasses that wave with each
passing wind. Interspersed with the grass are some hardy bushes and strong
trees, spread out like big lumps of green dropped onto the landscape. Small
animals dart in and out of the bushes creating little ruffles in the grass as
they run through. Occasionally a lone hawk or a flock of birds will cross the
sky.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Several young pines grow on this vast field, shooting off from the older trees
in the west. This is the perfect spot for new trees to grow, a large flourishing
field with nourishment from the river that passes by to the west. Many full and
round pinecones litter the ground, evidence that the trees are quite healthy. If
even half of the cones sprout this field could grow to be forest.
name: Pine Infested Landscape

desc:
This small trail near the riverbank is covered in high grass indicating it
hasn't seen much use in quite some time. The trail continues its journey
eastwards for a while, before coming to an abrupt halt before the fallen gates
of a ruined village. From the south the sound of rushing water can be heard,
presumably the roaring river to the west.
name: Abandoned Trail

desc:
Crossing the swift waters of the river below an old and creaking rope-bridge
passes the wild river here. Allowing safe passage for the worn and weary
traveller, the bridge safely carries anyone across as it bypasses the strong and
powerful currents and eddies that otherwise would be a harsh threat.
name: Old Rope Bridge

desc:
This small path is almost completely covered up by thick, waist-high grass. The
trail leads from a large river in the north and vanishes as it travels south. To
the west is a withered field of wheat, and a rushing river can be seen
south-east.
name: Vanishing Path

desc:
These swampy plains seem to have been created by a build up of runoff water from
the river which lies to the south-east. Frequent rains cause the river to swell,
flooding these plains over and over again. In the time it would take the ground
to absorb the previous flooding, the rains return creating an endless cycle of
watering. Due to this the grass here grows tall and wild, making shallow,
miniature waterways and paths. Various seeds float along in these channels
pushed along by the wind and the slope of the land leaving them to culminate in
little pools.
name: Soggy Plains

desc:
From this point one can see the beautiful vast plains that lie ahead.
Interspersed between the majestic stalks of the tall grass that grows here lies
a mixture of moss and weeds upon the ground. The mottled green and brown ground
cover gives this place a dismal tone. Every now and then, the ground causes an
uncomfortable squishing sensation, and if one stands still it feels as though
the ground is sinking.
name: Shaggy Old Plains

desc:
These plains sport a sharp gradient as if they were leaning towards something
far off to the south-east. The blowing wind across the tips of the grass creates
the illusion of ripples which carry the eye in that direction. Seemingly out of
place amidst these plains is a small group of pines jutting up straight out of
the ground, only to be pushed and bent by the winds. Small bushes dot the
landscape, providing shelter for rabbits and other small creatures.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
The vast sea of grass that lies here has slowly levelled off, with just a slight
slant towards the great river to the east. To the south and west the slopes are
much steeper, while to the east the plains are home to a wide variety of bushes.
The grade of the slope allows for a quite good view clear to the eastern
horizon.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
The forest in the west changes its shape as it makes its way towards the great
wilderness of the east. It slowly eases into a vast spread of grass and small
bushes. Little else about the lore of this land can be gleaned by examining the
surroundings. What hazardous creatures may live here is anyone's guess.
name: A Wimple at the Edge of the Wildlands

desc:
An ocean of grass spreads from this point, in the shadow of the large forest to
the west. The ground slopes gently down towards the east, where it eventually
meets the mighty river, Anduin. To the west looming trees stand, blocking any
further view. Far off east in the distance a little blue line, winding with
sweeping turns through the land, must be the Anduin, despite how small it looks
from this vantage point.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
These vast plains are covered with a brown cloak of grass, near death due to
lack of nutrients. The ground is littered with small rocks lying scattered
about. The plains slope down to the east, towards a large vale embracing the
great river Anduin. To the west the plains lie veiled in shadows. Southwards,
the large sloping plains continue for quite a distance until they meet the edge
of a river by the forest.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
These plains stretch for many miles to the east. In the evenings they fall under
the dark shadows of the Misty Mountains. Immediately south, a dark forest seems
to be encroaching upon these plains, as if attempting a conquest. Although a
small river can be seen to the west, the sounds of rushing water seem to be
coming from the south.
name: Shadowy Plains

desc:
The small river runs north-south here. A dark forest looms on its western bank.
Vast plains stretch before you to the east. Turning around, you see the high
peaks of the ominous Misty Mountains. The long mountain range stretches from
Angrenost, also known as Isengard, in the south, to Carn Dum, the capital of
Angmar, the land of the Witch-king, in the north. You have heard many
frightening tales about these mountains - about Moria, where no man dares to
travel, about Mount Gundabad, the orkish capital in the north, and about the
horrid goblins of the Goblin-gate, somewhere in the High Pass just west of here.
The river is less supported by water than it used to be and it slowly turns into
land.
name: An Old Riverbed

desc:
To the east of you is the dark pine forest which separates you from the foot of
the Misty Mountains. The small river emerges from the forest here, starting its
way east to the large river which eventually could bring you to Gondor and the
ocean beyond. The river is less supported by water than it used to be and it
slowly turns into land.
name: An Old Riverbed

desc:
A sombre silence lies upon this forest. Almost every life form seems to have
fled from the darkness that shrouds this place. The high, closely intertwined
treetops ensure that little light makes its way down to the forest floor.
However, there seems to be another, less obvious reason for the lingering
darkness.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The tunnel here is wet and muddy. It seems as though this passage was dug as
some sort of escape route, but perhaps a wall was dug too close to a stream,
since the tunnel is now flooded. This particular section of the passageway is
sloped upwards so the water is only about knee-deep. There is some sort of flat
stone above you, supporting the ceiling, but the tunnel to the east is totally
flooded. To proceed in that direction, one must plunge beneath the water, and
hope that the exit is not too far away.
name: Entrance to a Flooded Tunnel

desc:
This cottage may once have been nice. Now, however, the roof and walls are
shattered, fragmented remains of a home. The south wall is barely intact enough
to support the broken oak door, with its gold-coloured handle. In the centre of
this ruined place there is a small round table. A broken fireplace lies in what
used to be the northern wall.
name: Ruined Cottage

desc:
Some chairs sit here placed neatly around a table in the centre of the room. On
the walls you see an assortment of furs and animal heads, obviously trophies
belonging to the occupants of this hut. The animals represented are quite an
impressive collection, the prowess of this hunter must be almost legendary. You
can see a kitchen to the north, and south there is what looks like some sleeping
quarters.
name: Living Room

desc:
A black metal stove is the centrepiece of this kitchen, with a chimney climbing
the wall to speed the smoke on its way. On the wall a few shelves hold a large
variety of spices and seasonings. A large cupboard adorns the eastern wall, and
through the slightly open door you can see some choice cuts of meat hanging up
to cure.
name: Kitchen

desc:
Three straw pallets lie on the floor, and although they don't look extremely
comfortable, you guess that they must be much better than sleeping on the bare
floorboards. A chair sits in the corner, and a small lamp is resting on a little
wooden box in the middle of the southern wall. A large chest sits at the other
end of the room, and this must contain the possessions of the hunters who own
this abode.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
The narrow valley turns here, allowing you to proceed to the west or to the
south. A few tufts of grass grow along the path that is covered with footprints
of orcs.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The river rushes quickly, speeding away from its roots in the western forest.
Some leaves and twigs float in the water, little pieces of the forest it carries
with it like mementos of its origin. All this river has collected is swiftly
carried away from here, as the river continues on to some destination in the
east. Most of these seeds of the forest will be left along the shores of the
river before reaching the river's end.
name: Fast Flowing River

desc:
A high tree overlooking the dark landscape southeast and southwest. North it
brightens up a little where a small river calmly flows eastwards. Further north
large conifers builds up another dark forest from where strange sounds reach
this place now and then.
name: In a Tree

desc:
Large sturdy branches form the base of this great larch that is perfectly shaped
like an enormous evergreen tree. It quite possibly serves as a shelter for those
hiding from ferocious animals, or as a temporary lodging in the forest. The
hungry sounds of what might be wolves can be heard close by.
name: At the Top of a Tall Larch

desc:
You are standing on a thick branch. The thick mass of leaves blocks the sun out.
Far to the north is a small stream, to the east a road and beyond it a very dark
forest. The landscape to the south is not versatile, rocks and stones are
dominating. To the west there is a very steep mountain. Crunch... you stepped on
a little bone. Who uses the branch as a dining table?
name: In a Tree

desc:
You have found a small pit between the rocks. It is a perfect shelter for those
who like such environment. The walls of the pit are rocky. On the bottom there
is some wet soil between the stones. A damp smell fills the air.
name: A Pit

desc:
Looking south, you see the mountain range that separates the upper and lower
branches of the High Pass. To the west the trail is climbing up into the
mountains, and to the east it becomes wider. North of here is something that
looks like a hill.
name: Trail to the High Pass

desc:
The trail bends north and east here, around a large pillar of black rock
standing at the side of the road. In the east the trail continues further into
the range of mountains, and to the north the trail seems to make another sharp
bend. From this distance the pillar of rock appears to have been crudely carved
in the shape of a humanoid, wearing some sort of crown, but the ravages of the
weather make further detail impossible to see. To the south there is a rocky
path leading to a wooden hut.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
This cave offers you respite from the harsh wind and cold of the outdoors. But
what is this?.. the walls are covered in strange markings..like the claw marks
of some very large animal. Or maybe animals. A strange smell wafts from the east
and makes you want to gag. Perhaps you may just want to rest here then move on.
name: Entrance to a Cave

desc:
Pheeeew! This place really stinks. What sort of creatures could make this much
stench, you wonder. Looking at the floor, you can see piles of half-chewed
bones, many with shreds of putrefied flesh still attached. The cave extends to
the south, and from here you can hear some shuffling and scratching sounds.
Maybe it would be wise to leave before the inhabitants decide you will be the
next meal?
name: Inside the Smelly Cave

desc:
The stench in here is even worse than in the front of this cave, and suddenly
you see what left those bones out the front. The walls look like they have been
hewn out of the rock with the bare hands of the monsters that live in here.
peering into the gloom you can see the saliva dripping down the chins of these
trolls.."dinner is served" they could be saying.
name: Back of the Smelly Cave

desc:
The walls are made of closely fitted split logs, the floor laid in wooden
planks. The workmanship is good, so whoever built this place obviously knew how
to work with wood. The hut extends to the west, where you can see a room with a
few chairs and a table, obviously where the occupants spend most of the time
they are here.
name: Inside the Hut
